document_meta.recipient_line: All Member Banks, PSP Banks and UPI Applications
document_meta.subject_line: Subject: Product: ### 1. Feature The **Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion** Product Canvas Summary: ## Feature The **Dynamic Amount‑Suggestion** feature autom
document_meta.desired_outcome: Complete document suite for Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments

sections.heading: Letterhead & Reference Block
sections.content_instructions:
Prepare the issuing organization header details. Include only the minimum official identity elements needed for traceability.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement the UI to fetch and display amount suggestions from their transaction repository; must validate the final amount against existing UPI limits before sending ReqPay
  Issuer Banks: must continue to enforce PIN verification for amounts above the defined thresholds; must respond to standard UPI APIs without modification
  NPCI: will route ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails and related messages unchanged; will validate amount limits as per existing specifications

Go-Live Timeline: Not specified — omit dates
Supersedes: None
RULE: Terse, formal directive language. Use 'must' for mandatory, 'are advised to' for recommended.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
sections.prompt_instruction: Include the issuing organization name, a formal circular reference number in the format [ORG]/[DEPT]/OC No. [NNN]/[YYYY-YYYY], and the issue date flush-right. This header is mandatory and must appear before all body text.

sections.prompt_instruction: Write a subject line that names the action, the specific feature or artifact, and the system scope. Under 20 words. Formal sentence case.

sections.heading: Context Paragraph
sections.content_instructions:
Explain the current state, the ecosystem gap, and why the issuer is issuing this directive. Keep it factual and vendor-neutral.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment follows a fixed API sequence: the payer PSP sends a ReqPay, NPCI requests authentication details from the payee PSP, the payee validates and returns authentication, and finally the payer PSP confirms the transaction. The amount is entered manually by the user and is validated against static limits (e.g., >₹15,000 requires PIN, >₹500 for UPI‑Lite offline). No UI‑level suggestion of amounts exists in the protocol.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual entry of the transaction amount leads to longer payment times and higher probability of typographical errors. Payers also lack quick access to historically frequent amounts, which can cause friction especially for repeat contacts or merchants.
RULE: Terse, formal directive language. Use 'must' for mandatory obligations.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** We assume the **daily aggregate limits** remain the same as today (e.g., ₹1 Lakh per day for standard UPI, ₹10 K per day for UPI‑Lite) and will apply to transactions initiated via suggestions. Confirm, or specify separate daily limits for the suggestion feature.
A: yes

Q: **[Required]** To support the UI‑level suggestion capability we suggest adding a **new API message “GetAmountSuggestions”** (request: payerVPA, payeeVPA, maxSuggestions; response: up to three suggested_amounts, optional confidence_score). Confirm this design, or propose an alternative API name/structure (e.g., “FetchSuggestedAmounts”, additional fields, etc.).
A: yes continue with this
sections.prompt_instruction: Describe the current state, identify the limitation or opportunity, and briefly state what the issuer has decided to do in response. Single paragraph, 3-5 sentences.

sections.heading: Decision & Scope Statement
sections.content_instructions:
State the decision declaratively and name the technical artifacts, modules, schemas, or APIs affected.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestion for Payments.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The app shall retrieve the payer's last N transaction amounts for a given VPA from the PSP's transaction history.
  FR-02: The app shall display up to three amount suggestions derived from the retrieved history on the payment screen.
  FR-03: The payer shall be able to select a suggested amount with a single tap.
  FR-04: The payer shall be able to edit the suggested amount before confirming the payment.
  FR-05: The selected (or edited) amount shall be passed unchanged in the ReqPay message to NPCI.
  FR-06: The system shall enforce existing amount limits (₹15,000 for standard UPI, ₹500 for UPI‑Lite) on the suggested amount.
RULE: Terse, formal directive language. Use 'must' for mandatory obligations.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required]** We’re assuming the payer will be identified by a **Virtual Payment Address (VPA)**, which is the standard identifier used in existing UPI flows. Confirm, or specify an alternative identifier type (e.g., mobile number, Aadhaar‑linked ID).
A: yes use VPA

Q: **[Required]** We assume the payee will be identified by a **VPA (or merchant‑specific VPA/merchant ID)**, consistent with current person‑to‑person and merchant transactions. Confirm, or indicate if a different payee identifier (e.g., QR‑code ID, merchant code) is required.
A: yes continue with VPA

Q: **[Required]** For the Smart Amount Suggestions flow we propose to enforce the **existing per‑transaction limits** – ₹15,000 for standard UPI and ₹500 for UPI‑Lite – on any suggested amount. Confirm, or provide a distinct per‑transaction limit that should apply to suggested amounts.

A: yes continue with this
